mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and running workloads. It integrates seamlessly with Kubernetes to provide continuous security scanning and compliance checks. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configurations and potential performance impacts during scanning operations.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that configures the Trivy Operator's scanning behavior.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

```

> Creates a default TrivyConfiguration resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator is running correctly by listing the pods in the namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify the installation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the TrivyConfiguration and any scan results, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfigurations.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1' not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy CRDs may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/main/deploy/crds/trivy.aquasecurity.github.io_trivyconfigurations.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or unable to pull the image.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Scan results are not appearing
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not have permission to access the required resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the RBAC permissions are correctly set for the Trivy Operator.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matching manifest for linux/amd64' when pulling image
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image tag may not exist for your architecture.

**Fix:**
Verify the image tag and try a different version or architecture.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image repositories and improved error handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pod running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. The output of 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' should show the pod with the 'Running' status.

  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: docker.io/aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
